# Artifacts Review — 2026-07-23

**Date:** 2026-07-23
**Skill files reviewed:** 13
**Doc files reviewed:** 12 (excluding 5 generated files under `docs/reviews/`)
**Retros scanned:** 29
**Verdict:** Issues requiring action

> **Clean** = all alignment checks pass; only improvement opportunities found.
> **Minor issues** = inconsistencies present but none cause incorrect agent output.
> **Issues requiring action** = any inconsistency that would cause an agent to produce
>   incorrect output, or any unresolved High-priority retro action item.

---

## Executive Summary

All 33 High-priority retro action items targeting `docs/` or `.claude/skills/` are resolved — the
retro-driven improvement loop is working. The problem is elsewhere: several artifacts contain API and
type references that do not exist in `simulator/` or contradict the working `quizzes-full` code.
`docs/concepts/aggregate.md` § Factories tells agents to write `extends AggregateFactory<T>` — a type
that exists nowhere in the repository; `session-b.md` describes a `@CommandHandler`-annotation-per-command
handler design that does not match the framework's `handleDomainCommand(Command)` switch; `session-b.md`
and `session-d.md` call `unitOfWork.registerChanged(...)`, a method `UnitOfWork` does not have; and
`docs/concepts/rule-enforcement-patterns.md` § P2 still teaches the `subscribesEvent()` override that
retro-2.5.d and retro-2.7.d proved is a dead code path in the sagas profile. These are all Critical:
each one produces code that does not compile or silently does nothing.

The recommended next action is a single corrective pass over `docs/concepts/aggregate.md`,
`docs/concepts/rule-enforcement-patterns.md` § P2, `session-b.md`, and `session-d.md`, validating every
type and method signature against `simulator/` before re-committing. Every incorrect claim in this
report was verified against a file on disk.

---

## Check 1 — Path Validity

47 literal paths referenced across docs and skills resolve to real files. Paths written with an elision
(`applications/quizzes/src/main/java/.../execution/aggregate/Execution.java`) are deliberate shorthand
and are not treated as broken. The findings below are references whose non-elided component is wrong.

| Source file | Referenced path | Status | Notes |
|-------------|----------------|--------|-------|
| `docs/concepts/aggregate.md:20` | `simulator/src/main/java/pt/ulisboa/tecnico/socialsoftware/ms/domain/aggregate/Aggregate.java` | **Broken** | Fully-qualified, no elision. Actual: `simulator/src/main/java/pt/ulisboa/tecnico/socialsoftware/ms/aggregate/Aggregate.java` — there is no `ms/domain/` package. |
| `docs/architecture.md:22` | `simulator/.../ms/domain/aggregate/Aggregate.java` | **Broken** | Same wrong package segment `domain/`. |
| `docs/architecture.md:23`, `docs/concepts/aggregate.md:30`, `docs/concepts/sagas.md:11` | `simulator/.../ms/sagas/aggregate/SagaAggregate.java` | **Broken** | Actual: `ms/transaction/sagas/aggregate/SagaAggregate.java`. `aggregate.md:29` also states the FQN `ms.sagas.aggregate.SagaAggregate`, which is an import an agent would paste — actual FQN is `pt.ulisboa.tecnico.socialsoftware.ms.transaction.sagas.aggregate.SagaAggregate`. |
| `docs/architecture.md:24` | `simulator/.../ms/coordination/workflow/WorkflowFunctionality.java` | **Broken** | Actual: `ms/coordination/WorkflowFunctionality.java` — no `workflow/` segment. |
| `docs/concepts/sagas.md:12-15` | `ms/sagas/unitOfWork/SagaUnitOfWork.java`, `ms/sagas/unitOfWork/SagaUnitOfWorkService.java`, `ms/sagas/workflow/SagaWorkflow.java`, `ms/sagas/workflow/SagaStep.java` | **Broken** | All four are under `ms/transaction/sagas/...`. |
| `.claude/skills/boot-strap/SKILL.md:139` (File 4 Source) | `applications/quizzes/src/test/groovy/pt/ulisboa/tecnico/socialsoftware/SpockTest.groovy` | **Broken** | File is at `.../socialsoftware/quizzes/SpockTest.groovy`. The skill's own Step 2 item 3 gives the correct path and explicitly notes the discrepancy, then File 4 restates it wrongly. |

| all `applications/{app-name}/...`, `{tgt-src}...` forms | — | Template | Structure consistent with `docs/workflow.md` § Package layout, except as noted in Check 3. |

## Check 2 — Pattern Alignment (P1–P4 and R1–R8)

### P1–P4 Consistency

Authoritative definitions extracted from `docs/concepts/rule-enforcement-patterns.md` § Quick Reference:

- **P1** — single-aggregate state rule; `verifyInvariants()` in the aggregate; strong consistency.
- **P2** — cache state from another aggregate, no blocking; `getEventSubscriptions()` + event handler chain; eventual.
- **P3** — synchronous service-level check (own-table uniqueness OR saga-assembled DTO validation); guard in `*Service.java`; strong.
- **P4a** — precondition implicit in a saga fetch query; no enforcement code.
- **P4b** — invariant holds because the saga passes the same value to two aggregates; no enforcement code.

| Location | Pattern | Claim | Authoritative definition | Verdict |
|----------|---------|-------|--------------------------|---------|
| `docs/architecture.md:226-232` — pattern selection table | P1/P2/P3/P4a/P4b | Row text is verbatim-equivalent to the Quick Reference | as above | Consistent |
| `docs/architecture.md:129-143` — Request Lifecycle | P1/P2/P3 | `[P3]` service guard inside `@Transactional(SERIALIZABLE)` before mutation; `[P1]` at UoW commit; `[P2]` async ~1 s | matches § Enforcement Lifecycle | Consistent |
| `.claude/skills/classify-and-plan/SKILL.md:195-234` — Step 4 | P1–P4a/b | Explicitly defers to `rule-enforcement-patterns.md` § Decision Guide; keyword matcher declared "mechanical only … not part of the doc's decision criteria"; order same-aggregate → P4a/P4b → P3 → P2 | matches flowchart order | Consistent |
| `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-a.md:54` | P1 | "`verifyInvariants()`: enforces all **P1 rules** … Throws `{AppClass}Exception`" | P1 = `verifyInvariants()` | Consistent |
| `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-b.md:59-60` | P3 | "P3 own-table uniqueness guards" + "P3 DTO field checks … receive the saga-assembled DTO as a parameter" | both P3 sub-cases | Consistent |
| `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-d.md:114` | P2 | "**P2 rule enforcement:** The invariant check happens inside the Functionalities update method … calls `verifyInvariants()` before committing" | P2 "may only cache state … no operation is blocked" | Consistent but loosely worded — what fires is the consumer's own **P1** check after the cache update, not a P2 check. Recommend renaming the heading to "P2 cache update — invariant re-check". Minor. |
| `.claude/skills/review-aggregate/SKILL.md:240-246` — Step 6 | P1/P2/P3/P4a/P4b | Per-classification checks match each pattern's implementation site | as above | Consistent |
| `.claude/skills/adversarial-review-aggregate/SKILL.md:113-137` — Step 3 | P1 | Family A falsifies `verifyInvariants()` predicates, excludes Java-`final` rules | matches | Consistent |
| `.claude/skills/adversarial-review-aggregate/SKILL.md:257-276` — Step 8 | Family F | Re-derives from "§ Decision Guide and the domain model text alone", before reading plan.md | matches | Consistent |

**Inconsistency found inside the authoritative doc itself** — `docs/concepts/rule-enforcement-patterns.md`
§ P2 Implementation recipe contradicts `docs/concepts/events.md` and `session-d.md` on two points. See
Action Items C4 and M1.

### R1–R8 Coverage

Authoritative one-liners from `docs/architecture.md` § Architectural Restrictions. Family D of
`.claude/skills/adversarial-review-aggregate/SKILL.md` Step 6 (lines 196-205) is the single
code-conformance owner.

| Restriction | Expected location | In Family D table? | Mentioned? | Consistent? | Notes |
|-------------|------------------|--------------------|------------|-------------|-------|
| R1 — service loads only own aggregate | `docs/concepts/service.md`, `session-b.md` | Yes | Partial | Partial | `service.md:35` says "Never inject a foreign service class or a foreign repository — see [R1, R2 in architecture.md]" — that sentence describes **R2**; R1 (only `aggregateLoadAndRegisterRead` your own type) is never stated in `service.md`. Not mentioned in `session-b.md`. Family D's check is correct. |
| R2 — service injects only own components | `docs/concepts/service.md`, `session-b.md` | Yes | Yes | Consistent | `service.md` § Injected Dependencies lists the exact allowed set. But `session-b.md:224` bean template says "inject repos and other services as needed" — see Action Item C3. |
| R3 — cross-aggregate flow via DTOs only | `docs/concepts/service.md`, `session-b.md` | Yes | **No** | n/a | R3 appears nowhere outside `architecture.md` and Family D. `service.md` covers DTO immutability (R7) but never states that a service must not hold a foreign concrete aggregate type. |
| R4 — `setForbiddenStates` on mutating saga steps | `docs/concepts/sagas.md`, `session-b.md` | Yes | Yes | Consistent | `sagas.md` § R4 Decision Table; `session-b.md:97` defers to it by name. |
| R5 — `getEventSubscriptions()` in consumer only | `docs/concepts/events.md`, `session-d.md` | Yes | By concept, not by label | Consistent | Upstream/downstream rule stated in `rule-enforcement-patterns.md` § P2 ("`getEventSubscriptions()` always lives in the downstream (consumer) aggregate"), which `architecture.md` R5 points to. Neither `events.md` nor `session-d.md` names R5. |
| R6 — `verifyInvariants()` must not read from DB | `docs/concepts/aggregate.md`, `session-a.md` | Yes | **No** | n/a | Neither `aggregate.md` § getEventSubscriptions/§ Base Class nor `session-a.md` tells the author of `verifyInvariants()` that repository access is forbidden. An agent writes the method from `session-a.md:54` and never sees the restriction. |
| R7 — DTOs are immutable value objects | `docs/concepts/service.md`, `session-b.md` | Yes | Yes | Consistent | `service.md` § DTO Immutability (R7); `session-b.md:16` reads list names it. |
| R8 — functionalities only send commands upstream | `docs/concepts/commands.md`, `session-b.md` | Yes | Yes (in `sagas.md`, not `commands.md`) | Consistent | `sagas.md:269` "**R8 — upstream-only commands**"; `session-b.md:97` names it. `commands.md` never mentions it — acceptable, since the rule is about saga step construction. |

All eight restrictions are present in the Family D table and each check matches the authoritative
one-liner. No Critical coverage gap. R3 and R6 are documented in `architecture.md` and enforced by the
adversarial pass, but are absent from the concept doc an implementer actually reads mid-session — see
Action Items M4 and M5.

## Check 3 — Improvement Opportunities

### Missing Examples

| Doc | Section | Why an example would help |
|-----|---------|---------------------------|
| `docs/concepts/aggregate.md` | § Repositories | Prose only, and the prose is wrong (see C2). A 6-line `{Aggregate}CustomRepositorySagas` example — `@Service @Profile("sagas")`, `implements {Aggregate}CustomRepository`, `@Autowired {Aggregate}Repository` — would both fix the error and remove the need to look at another aggregate. |
| `docs/architecture.md` | R3, R8 | R1, R2 and R6 each carry an "**Instead:**" paragraph showing the correct alternative; R3 and R8 do not. A one-line violating snippet plus its fix would make each checkable at a glance. |
| `docs/concepts/commands.md` | § Routing Commands | The `serviceName + "CommandHandler"` bean-name rule is stated in prose. The `BeanConfigurationSagas` `@Bean` method that must satisfy it is never shown, and `session-b.md`'s bean template (line 228) uses a generic `{aggregate}CommandHandler` name without repeating the camelCase constraint. Show the `@Bean quizAnswerCommandHandler()` case. |
| `docs/concepts/aggregate.md` | § Factories | The example shows the interface and the sagas implementation but not how `BeanConfigurationSagas` registers `Sagas{Aggregate}Factory` while the service injects `{Aggregate}Factory`. That indirection is the whole point of the profile-agnostic design in `AGENTS.md`. |
| `docs/concepts/service.md` | § Custom Repository — Latest-Active-Version Query | Shows the JPQL but not the `{Aggregate}CustomRepositorySagas` method that calls it, which is where the `aggregateLoadAndRegisterRead` mapping loop lives (described in `session-c.md:52` in prose only). |

### Ambiguous Guidance

| File | Step | Ambiguous phrase | Missing decision criterion |
|------|------|------------------|---------------------------|
| `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-b.md` | Update BeanConfigurationSagas (line 224) | `{Aggregate}Service {aggregate}Service(...) { // inject repos and other services as needed` | "other services" has no criterion and directly invites an R2 violation. `docs/concepts/service.md` § Injected Dependencies gives the closed list; the template should point there and say "own repository, own custom repository, `UnitOfWorkService` — never a foreign service". |
| `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-b.md` | Produce → `{Op}FunctionalitySagas.java` (line 95) | "Extends `FunctionalitySagas` (**or the appropriate saga base class from the simulator core**)" | No such class as `FunctionalitySagas` exists; the base is `WorkflowFunctionality`. The parenthetical converts a wrong name into an open-ended guess. See C5. |
| `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-c.md` | Produce → `{Query}FunctionalitySagas.java` (line 69) | "Extends **the read-functionality base class from simulator core**" | Never named. `docs/concepts/sagas.md` § Read Functionality Sagas shows `extends WorkflowFunctionality`; state it here. |
| `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-a.md` | `{Aggregate}CustomRepositorySagas.java` (line 138) | "Add custom JPQL query method implementations only **as needed**" | Acceptable — the following clause gives the concrete fallback ("for aggregates with no cross-table lookups, the class body can be left empty"). No action. |
| `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-b.md` | `{Aggregate}CommandHandler.java` (line 78) | "One `@CommandHandler` method per command class produced in this session" | Not ambiguous — wrong. See C1. |

## Action Items

| Priority | Category | File | Finding | Suggested Fix |
|----------|---------|------|---------|---------------|
| Critical | Non-existent type | `docs/concepts/aggregate.md:49,69` | § Factories declares `public interface CourseFactory extends AggregateFactory<Course>` and states "Factories implement `AggregateFactory<T>`". `grep -rn AggregateFactory simulator applications` returns **zero matches** — the type does not exist. The real `applications/quizzes-full/.../course/aggregate/CourseFactory.java` is `public interface CourseFactory {` with no supertype, matching `session-a.md:107` ("Plain Java interface — no annotations"). An agent following `aggregate.md` writes a non-compiling file. **(C2)** | Delete `extends AggregateFactory<Course>` from the snippet and delete the sentence "Factories implement `AggregateFactory<T>` and are injected into services via the interface" — replace with "Factories are plain interfaces, injected into services via the interface — never the concrete `Sagas*` class." |
| Critical | Wrong framework shape | `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-b.md:77-80` | § `{Aggregate}CommandHandler.java` states "One `@CommandHandler` method per command class produced in this session" and "Each handler: creates a `UnitOfWork`, calls the matching service method, **commits the `UnitOfWork`**, returns result". No `@CommandHandler` annotation exists in `simulator/`. The real `CourseCommandHandler` extends `CommandHandler`, overrides `getAggregateTypeName()` and a single `handleDomainCommand(Command)` switch, and takes the UoW from `cmd.getUnitOfWork()` — it never creates or commits one. `docs/concepts/commands.md:116-145` and `review-aggregate/SKILL.md:187-190` both describe the correct shape, so session-b.md is the sole outlier. **(C1)** | Replace the three bullets with a pointer to `docs/concepts/commands.md` § Routing Commands (CommandHandler): `@Component`, `extends CommandHandler`, `getAggregateTypeName()` returns PascalCase, one `case` per command in `handleDomainCommand`, mutating cases `yield null`, `default` logs a warning. Remove all mention of `@CommandHandler` and of creating/committing a UoW in the handler. |
| Critical | Non-existent API | `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-b.md:61,63`; `session-d.md:63,147` | Four call sites instruct `unitOfWork.registerChanged(aggregate)` / `unitOfWork.registerEvent(new {Event}(...))`. `simulator/.../unitOfWork/UnitOfWork.java` exposes only `getId/setId`, `getVersion/setVersion`, `getFunctionalityName/setFunctionalityName`, `getAggregatesToCommit`, `getEventsToEmit`, `addEvent` — there is no `registerChanged` and no `registerEvent`. The real API is on the service: `unitOfWorkService.registerChanged(aggregate, unitOfWork)` and `unitOfWorkService.registerEvent(event, unitOfWork)`, as used throughout `docs/concepts/service.md` and `CourseService`. **(C3)** | Replace all four with the two-argument `unitOfWorkService.*` forms. Same fix in the `session-d.md` code blocks under § Shared-anchor events and § UpdateQuestionEvent. |
| Critical | Contradicts corrected doc | `docs/concepts/rule-enforcement-patterns.md:157-175` | § P2 Step 1's subscription-class template overrides `subscribesEvent()` and puts the discriminating check inside it. `docs/concepts/events.md:52,163` and `session-d.md:47` all state that in the sagas profile `EventApplicationService.handleSubscribedEvent()` does **not** call `subscribesEvent()`, so this filter silently never runs — the exact defect retro-2.5.d and retro-2.7.d were raised to eliminate. It survived in this file because the retros targeted `events.md` and `session-d.md` only. **(C4)** | Delete the `subscribesEvent()` override from the P2 Step 1 template. Replace with the two-constructor form from `events.md` § Canonical Wiring Snippet → Subscription class, plus the one-line note that additional filtering belongs in the service-layer ByEvent method. |
| Critical | Wrong base class | `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-b.md:95` | "Extends `FunctionalitySagas` (or the appropriate saga base class from the simulator core)". No `FunctionalitySagas` class exists; `docs/concepts/sagas.md` § Write Workflow Structure and `review-aggregate/SKILL.md:198` both say `extends WorkflowFunctionality` with a `buildWorkflow(...)` method. The next bullet ("Steps are defined as `SagaStep` instances in the constructor or `defineSteps()` method") also names a non-existent `defineSteps()`. **(C5)** | Replace both bullets with: "Extends `WorkflowFunctionality`; the constructor calls `buildWorkflow(...)`, which creates `new SagaWorkflow(this, unitOfWorkService, unitOfWork)` and adds `SagaStep`s — see `docs/concepts/sagas.md` § Write Workflow Structure." Apply the same fix to `session-c.md:69`. |
| Major | Broken package paths | `docs/architecture.md:22-24`; `docs/concepts/aggregate.md:20,29-30`; `docs/concepts/sagas.md:11-15` | Nine simulator class paths use packages that do not exist: `ms/domain/aggregate/` (actual `ms/aggregate/`), `ms/sagas/...` (actual `ms/transaction/sagas/...`), `ms/coordination/workflow/` (actual `ms/coordination/`). `aggregate.md:29` gives the wrong FQN `ms.sagas.aggregate.SagaAggregate` as an import target. | Correct all nine to the verified paths listed in Check 1. |
| Major | Inconsistent naming convention | `.claude/skills/review-tests/SKILL.md:110`; `.claude/skills/review-aggregate/SKILL.md:268` | Both state the T4 file convention as `{Operation}{Aggregate}Test.groovy`. Actual files include `AddParticipantTest.groovy`, `AnonymizeStudentTest.groovy`, `DisenrollStudentTest.groovy` — the aggregate name is not appended. `docs/concepts/testing.md` (`<FunctionalityName>Test`) and `docs/workflow.md` (`{Operation}Test.groovy`) are correct. A review run against the wrong convention reports existing tests as Missing. | Change both to `{FunctionalityName}Test.groovy` and cite `docs/concepts/testing.md` § Directory Layout as the owner. |
| Major | R2 violation invited | `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-b.md:224` | Bean template comment reads "inject repos **and other services** as needed". R2 forbids injecting a foreign service into `{Aggregate}Service`; `docs/concepts/service.md` § Injected Dependencies gives the closed allowed list. | Change the comment to "inject own repository / custom repository and `UnitOfWorkService` only — see `docs/concepts/service.md` § Injected Dependencies (R2)". |
| Major | Contradicts corrected doc | `docs/concepts/rule-enforcement-patterns.md:192-201` | § P2 Step 3's polling snippet constructs a per-event handler: `handleSubscribedEvent({EventName}.class, new {EventName}Handler(repository, {aggregate}EventProcessing))`. `docs/concepts/events.md:62` states "Use **one concrete `{Consumer}EventHandler` class** per consumer aggregate … Do **not** create a separate handler subclass per event type", and `session-d.md:82` agrees. **(M1)** | Replace the snippet with the `events.md` § Polling method form: `handleSubscribedEvent(<EventName>.class, {consumer}EventHandler)` using the shared autowired bean. |
| Major | Broken references | `.claude/skills/boot-strap/SKILL.md:139`; `.claude/skills/classify-and-plan/SKILL.md:22` | boot-strap File 4 gives the SpockTest source as `.../socialsoftware/SpockTest.groovy` (actual: `.../socialsoftware/quizzes/SpockTest.groovy`), contradicting its own Step 2 item 3. classify-and-plan's usage example cites `docs/examples/example-domain-model.md` and `docs/examples/example-aggregate-grouping.md`; `docs/examples/` does not exist. | Fix the boot-strap path to match Step 2. Replace the classify-and-plan example with the real `applications/quizzes/quizzes-domain-model.md` / `quizzes-aggregate-grouping.md` pair, or create `docs/examples/`. |
| Minor | Assertion-form inconsistency | `session-a.md:164`; `docs/concepts/testing.md:190`; `review-tests/SKILL.md:180,231` | Three different assertion forms are prescribed for the same check: `ex.getErrorMessage() == {RULE_NAME}` (session-a.md, review-tests Step 6), `ex.message == <RULE_NAME>` (testing.md T1/T2 templates, review-tests Step 5.C), and `docs/concepts/service.md` § Exception-Throw Convention which asserts the convention is `ex.message == CONSTANT`. **(M2)** | Pick one — `ex.message ==` matches the majority and the Exception-Throw Convention rationale — and make `docs/concepts/testing.md` the single owner, with the other files pointing at it. |
| Minor | Layout divergence | `docs/workflow.md:26-62` vs `docs/architecture.md:55-91` | workflow.md's package tree omits `coordination/webapi/` (the `{Xxx}Controller` stub) and `notification/api/`, both present in architecture.md's canonical layout and both produced by `session-b.md` § `{Aggregate}Controller.java`. | Add the two directories to workflow.md's tree, or replace the tree with a pointer to `docs/architecture.md` § Package Structure Convention as the single owner. |
| Minor | Doc coverage gap (R6) | `docs/concepts/aggregate.md`, `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-a.md` | R6 ("`verifyInvariants()` must not perform DB reads") appears only in `architecture.md` and the adversarial Family D table. The agent writing `verifyInvariants()` reads `session-a.md:54` and `aggregate.md`, neither of which mentions it. **(M4)** | Add a one-line restriction under `session-a.md`'s `verifyInvariants()` bullet and under `aggregate.md` § Base Class, citing R6 and pointing to the P3 alternative. |
| Minor | Doc coverage gap (R3) | `docs/concepts/service.md` | R3 ("cross-aggregate state must flow through DTOs, not aggregate instances") is stated nowhere outside `architecture.md` and Family D. `service.md` covers R7 (DTO immutability) but not R3. Separately, `service.md:35` attributes the injection rule to "R1, R2" when it describes R2 only. **(M5)** | Add an R3 sentence to `service.md` § Injected Dependencies or § DTO Immutability, and correct the `[R1, R2 …]` citation to state R1 separately (own-type `aggregateLoadAndRegisterRead` only). |
| Minor | Loose pattern labelling | `.claude/skills/implement-aggregate/session-d.md:114` | Heading "**P2 rule enforcement:**" describes the consumer's own `verifyInvariants()` firing after a cached-field update — that is a P1 check, and `rule-enforcement-patterns.md` § Step 3 is explicit that P2 never blocks. | Rename to "P2 cache update — consumer invariant re-check" and note that the check that fires is the consumer's P1. |

**Critical** = causes an agent to produce incorrect output (wrong code, missing file, wrong beans,
  wrong exception type, broken skill delegation).
**Major** = causes unnecessary reference-app lookups, or is an unresolved High-priority retro item.
**Minor** = cosmetic inconsistency, missing example, ambiguous wording with low impact.
